We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
coordinate with the GSO to determine the appropriate useful lives of the PPEs with no estimated useful lives and date of acquisition and provide for the appropriate deprecation;
prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the understatement of Accumulated Depreciation and Depreciation Expense for 2024; and
judiciously review the schedule of depreciation and determine the discrepancies with the financial statements to ensure accuracy and consistency in reporting under IPSASs 1 and 17.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reflect the receivables at net realizable value and provide allowance for impairment for its receivable accounts which are considered to be impaired considering the probability of actual collection the LGU expects from these receivables in order to achieve faithful representation in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to (1) provide adequate disclosure for the Net Assets/Equity, Subsidy to Other Funds; and Subsidies – Others accounts and the differences between the original and final budgets reflected in the SCBAA caused by the enactment of supplemental budgets and (2) trace the discrepancy between the reciprocal accounts – Subsidy to Other Funds and Subsidy from Other Funds and record the appropriate correcting entry; and
Municipal Budget Officer to maintain detailed records vis-à-vis the Municipality’s appropriations, allotments, obligations and balances that could be vouched to specific transactions and reconcile the same with the Municipal Accountant’s SCBAA in order to provide consensus between the records.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and determine the appropriate Discount on Advance Payment of RPT and SET and recognize the same in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to determine the specific building/s that is/are held for rentals and the corresponding cost and accumulated depreciation and reclassify them to the Investment Property account pursuant to IPSAS 1 and the Revised Chart of Accounts and to present fairly the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the journal entries to correct the error in recording and religiously follow IPSAS 1 and the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in recording financial transactions in order to properly depict accurate financial information and financial statements that are free from material error.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO to:
prepare the SSMI on a monthly basis and submit a copy to the Accounting Department to facilitate timely recording of adjusting entries for supplies and materials issued and to properly recognize them in the period in which these were consumed/distributed; and
maintain a complete set of stock cards for all items of inventory in order to properly monitor and report the issuance of supplies and materials and should be reconciled with the RISs to facilitate the verification of the entries made by the Accounting Department.

We recommended that Management:
establish and implement an effective internal control in the Municipality especially the payment function;
direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to scrutinize all invoices sent by suppliers as evidence of payments for their completeness and accuracy and not to accept altered invoices in order to establish the legality and propriety of the payments; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check which payee is the business name and not the individual as the Municipality is transacting with the business.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
assign a new waste materials property inspector who is not the Supply Officer or any employee of the GSO to avoid incompatible functions; and
instruct the Supply/Property Custodian and waste materials property inspector to ensure that WMRs indicate the proper mode of disposal of waste materials and warrant the eventual disposition of such waste materials.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC and end-users to abide by Section 7.3.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on procurement planning, and that the end users should include those specifications that actually meet their needs and requirements in every procurement in order to establish efficiency and avoid unnecessary spending.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to ensure that all RFQs sent to suppliers are duly approved by the BAC and all the requirements in the RFQs were properly complied with before recommending the award of the contract to the supplier in order to establish the responsiveness of the quotations pursuant to Section 53.9 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Municipal Budget Officer in coordination with the Local Accountant, Local Treasurer and Planning and Development Coordinator, monitor and evaluate budget performance of all PPAs; and
Local Finance Committee to conduct a semi-annual review and general examination of expenditures and actual accomplishments against performance standards in undertaking development projects in order to properly and effectively monitor the status of the municipality’s PPAs, and provide a report to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) of the status of implementation of PPAs based on the review conducted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management abide strictly with pertinent laws, rules and
regulations on controls over PPE:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Supply Officer to reconcile their
records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balance of each PPE
account presented in the financial statement.
2. Require the Inventory Committee to provide tagging of all PPEs (property
identification and coding) pursuant to Property and Supply Management
Manual.
3. Instruct the Supply Officer to replace all Memorandum Receipts (MRs)
issued with Property Acknowledgement Receipts (PARs) on all government
properties to establish accountability and to easy monitor properties.
4. Require the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain Property
Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, for each class of property.
5. Require the Supply Officer to exclude small tangible items with serviceable
life of more than one year from the RCPPE, and prepare ICS. Likewise,
require the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the PPE
accounts to exclude small tangible items.

We recommended that management require the GSO Designate to update his
records and prepare property transfer report and for Municipal Accountant to
derecognize the properties issued to zanjeras and barangays.

We recommended that management adhere to the use of the Philippine Drug Price
Reference Index (DPRI) set forth by the Department of Health in order to achieve
best value for money in sourcing essential medicines.

We recommended that management adhere to the documentary requirements
cited in COA Circular 2012-001 in the procurement of drugs and medicines to ensure
that items procured originated from legitimate sources and are effective as well as
genuine.

Moreover, we recommended for the submission of documentary requirements
necessary to validate the claims.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit
in our office the necessary documentary requirements as mentioned in the above COA
Circular to avoid possible suspension.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer to integrate the Construction Safety and
Health Program in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be
implemented to ensure the protection and welfare of the general public within and
around the immediate vicinity of any construction worksite as well as the promotion of
harmonious employer-employee relationships in the construction industry.

Also, we recommended that Bids and Awards Committee require Contractors to
submit DOLE-approved CSHP to ensure the latter’s compliance with Occupational
Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE.

We recommended that management invariably comply with Section 62.2.2 of the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 requiring the issuance of Certificate of Acceptance to the
contractor after the lapse of the defects liability period or one year from project
completion to final acceptance and upon submission of the warranty security by the
contractor.

We recommended that management instruct the drivers to complete the data
required in the Daily Trip Tickets, particularly supporting the computation of fuel
consumption in the trip tickets such as odometer readings, places to visit/inspect and
number of liters of fuel consumed.

Also, we recommended that management place the required markings in all
motor vehicles of the municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended that management ensure that relevant information in all
infrastructure projects are posted in tarpaulin or similar materials to inform the public
of the projects implemented by the Municipality.

c. General Services Officer designate to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

and intelligence expenses be submitted directly to the COA Chairman pursuant to
COA Circular NO. 2003-003.

We have recommended the immediate liquidation of the outstanding cash
advances and the management to cause the withholding of salary of the concerned
personnel as required in COA Circular No. 97-002. Furthermore, we reiterate the
accounting department to ensure that no additional cash advances will be granted
without liquidating the previous cash advance.

We have recommended the management to maximize the utilization of the
20% development fund and RA 7171 Fund allocated for the implementation of
various programs and projects that are envisioned to help the constituents and
farmers in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development. If
the programs and projects are no longer feasible, then the funds be reverted and
re-appropriated only for 20% MDF and RA 7171 in accordance with laws, rules
and regulations.

We have recommended that in the appropriation of the proceeds of the SEF,
the Local School Board to consider only those programs, projects and activities that
are responsive to the purposes stated in Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160.

We have recommended the management to require the immediate submission of liquidation papers of all officials and employees with outstanding cash advances so that the settlements can be effected, direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold salaries of accountable officers with outstanding balances and to suspend the practice of granting additional cash advance if the previous cash advance is still unliquidated.
